paper_title: No Resource, No Benchmarks, No Problem? Evaluating and Improving LLMs for Code Generation in No-Resource Languages

research_seeds.problem: Pre-training + instruction transferring still leaves syntactic errors (especially for smaller models).
research_seeds.hypothesis: Enforcing the formal grammar of the target language at inference time via constrained decoding (e.g., XGrammar) could eliminate syntactic errors entirely, providing a complementary boost to training-based approaches.
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Apply XGrammar on Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B Base pre-trained on Gleam; compare against current best (instruction transferring) on McEval-Hard. Expected: syntactic errors eliminated → semantic correctness becomes the only failure mode.
research_seeds.expected_impact: High — directly addresses the primary failure mode for no-resource languages

research_seeds.seed_name: Cross-Language Transfer via Syntax Tree Similarity
research_seeds.problem: The paper notes Lua's similarity to Python helps performance, but no systematic measure is applied.
research_seeds.hypothesis: A syntax-tree overlap metric between a no-resource language and known languages predicts how quickly/well LLMs can adapt. This could inform language selection for deployment and data augmentation strategies.
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Compute AST overlap (e.g., tree-edit distance weighted by node type frequency) between Gleam/MoonBit and Python/Java/Rust. Correlate with per-language performance gains from pre-training. Augment Gleam data with Rust examples.
research_seeds.expected_impact: Medium — improves understanding and may guide data augmentation
key_references: This paper's Lua vs. R observation (§III)
Chen et al. [9] on language similarity transfer

research_seeds.seed_name: Beyond Function-Level: Real-World Task Benchmarks for No-Resource Languages
research_seeds.problem: Current benchmarks are function-level translations from high-resource languages. Real-world usage involves debugging, refactoring, and change-request-level tasks.
research_seeds.hypothesis: The performance gap between no-resource and high-resource languages widens further on real-world tasks, or conversely, the benefit of pre-training is larger because real tasks exercise more language features.
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Collect 50-100 real GitHub issues from Gleam/MoonBit repositories, create natural language specifications, and evaluate LLMs with instruction transferring vs. zero-shot on 'implement this feature' or 'fix this bug' tasks.
research_seeds.expected_impact: High — validates real-world applicability; could establish a new benchmark standard

research_seeds.seed_name: Synthetic Data Generation for No-Resource Languages via DSL-to-General Translation
research_seeds.problem: Limited data (~13-28M tokens) constrains pre-training effectiveness.
research_seeds.hypothesis: If a no-resource language can be modeled as a DSL (domain-specific language), a model-to-text transformation from a semantically compatible high-resource language could generate synthetic training data (inspired by ModelMate).
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Define a semantic mapping from Python common patterns to Gleam patterns. Use GPT-4o to generate synthetic Gleam code from Python equivalents. Validate syntactically and add to pre-training corpus. Measure incremental improvement.
research_seeds.expected_impact: Medium-High — could substantially increase training data

research_seeds.seed_name: Weight Diff Transfer Across Model Architectures
research_seeds.problem: Current approach requires an instruct and base model with the same architecture and size. This limits applicability.
research_seeds.hypothesis: Using representation alignment (e.g., CCA on hidden states) or LoRA adapters, instruction following capabilities might be transferable across different model architectures.
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Train a small probe on the weight diff representation space; attempt to generate architecture-agnostic instruction-following vectors. Test on models from different families (e.g., apply Qwen diff to Llama).
research_seeds.expected_impact: Very High — would dramatically expand the technique's applicability

research_seeds.seed_name: Long-Term Tracking: The No-Resource Gap Over LLM Generations
research_seeds.problem: As LLMs evolve and incorporate more training data, the 'no-resource' status may shift. It's unclear how persistent the gap is.
research_seeds.hypothesis: The gap between high-resource and no-resource languages narrows with each LLM generation, but the narrowing rate depends on architectural advances (e.g., reasoning models may generalize better).
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Repeat the zero-shot evaluation (RQ1) on each new LLM generation (GPT-5, Qwen 4, etc.) using the same benchmarks. Track the ratio: pass@1(no-resource) / pass@1(high-resource) over time.
research_seeds.expected_difficulty: Low (just run existing benchmarks on new models)
research_seeds.expected_impact: Medium — valuable for practitioners deciding when to invest in custom solutions vs. waiting for general LLM improvement

research_seeds.seed_name: Instruction Transferring Without a Base-Instruct Pair
research_seeds.problem: Many models don't have both base and instruct versions publicly available.
research_seeds.hypothesis: Instruction following capabilities can be distilled into a pre-trained model using synthetic instruction-following data, without requiring the exact Mi - Mb weight diff.
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Generate synthetic instruction-following pairs using GPT-4o for Gleam code generation. Fine-tune the pre-trained base model on this synthetic data. Compare against weight diff transfer.
research_seeds.expected_impact: High — removes the strict requirement of matched base/instruct model pairs

research_seeds.seed_name: Multi-Language Pre-Training: Can Learning One No-Resource Language Help Another?
research_seeds.problem: Each no-resource language is studied in isolation. Languages share programming concepts (loops, recursion, pattern matching).
research_seeds.hypothesis: Pre-training on multiple no-resource languages simultaneously creates positive transfer — shared concepts reinforce each other, improving performance on each individual language beyond single-language pre-training.
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Pre-train Qwen 2.5 Coder 32B Base on both Gleam and MoonBit data simultaneously. Compare pass@1 against models pre-trained on each language separately.
research_seeds.expected_impact: Medium — could inform deployment strategy for companies with multiple proprietary languages

research_seeds.seed_name: Active Learning for No-Resource Language Benchmark Construction
research_seeds.problem: Manual benchmark translation cost 340 man-hours. Scaling to more languages is prohibitive.
research_seeds.hypothesis: An active learning pipeline can identify the most informative coding tasks for translation, reducing human effort while maintaining benchmark coverage of language features.
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Build an active learning algorithm that: (1) analyzes language features needed for each coding task; (2) selects minimal task subset covering all features; (3) prioritizes tasks most likely to reveal LLM weaknesses. Validate against full HumanEval translation.
research_seeds.expected_impact: Medium — could enable scaling to many more no-resource languages

research_seeds.problem: MoonBit outperforms Gleam in most settings, with the paper attributing this partly to 'AI-friendly design.' No systematic definition exists.
research_seeds.hypothesis: Certain language design choices (consistent syntax, minimal keywords, unambiguous parsing, Python-like comprehensibility) correlate with LLM code generation performance. This could inform how organizations design their proprietary languages.
research_seeds.minimum_viable_experiment: Survey 10-20 programming language features, rate them on 'AI-friendliness' criteria (consistent grammar, low ambiguity, common patterns with high-resource languages). Correlate with zero-shot pass@1 across all 9 languages in this paper.
research_seeds.expected_impact: High — actionable guidance for language designers in industry
